playbooks.title: Fundraising Trigger
playbooks.subtitle: Series C preparation pipeline
playbooks.description: Structured path from ARR milestone to Series C close. Each step has a data gate — no gut feelings.

costOfInaction.description: Delayed Series C = $42K/mo in valuation gap growth
costOfInaction.basis: ARR growth vs static valuation multiple erosion

steps.title: Fix burn multiple below 2×
steps.description: Reduce SMB acquisition spend. Focus on enterprise where LTV:CAC is 6.2× vs SMB's 2.8×

recommendations.label: Cut SMB acquisition budget 30%
recommendations.detail: SMB CAC payback is 22mo vs Enterprise's 8mo. Redirect $126K/yr to enterprise channel.

recommendations.label: Shift 2 SDRs from SMB to Enterprise
recommendations.detail: Enterprise pipeline coverage improves from 2.8× to 3.4×. SMB pipeline already over-served.

recommendations.label: Raise SMB pricing 20%
recommendations.impact: Burn multiple drops to 1.9×, some churn risk
recommendations.detail: SMB NRR is 96% (below breakeven). Price-sensitive segment, expect 8-12% churn spike.

playbooks.title: Hiring Trigger
playbooks.subtitle: Sales team expansion gate
playbooks.description: Data-gated hiring. Pipeline coverage, rep productivity, and CFO scenario check must all pass before offers go out.

steps.title: CFO scenario check
steps.description: Bear scenario must show ≥12mo runway with planned hires factored in.
steps.blockReason: Bear scenario shows 11.8mo runway with Plan hiring. Below 12mo minimum.

resolutionOptions.label: Defer hiring 8 weeks
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Pipeline coverage may drop to 2.6× without new reps

resolutionOptions.label: Hire 2 instead of 3
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Slower revenue ramp, ~$180K/yr less new ARR capacity

resolutionOptions.label: Cut marketing spend 15%
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Top-of-funnel lead volume drops ~20% in 2 months

playbooks.title: Pivot / Strategic Direction
playbooks.subtitle: Go/no-go on changing the growth engine
playbooks.description: When core growth metrics plateau for 2+ quarters despite execution, evaluate whether the current strategy can reach the next milestone. This playbook gates the pivot decision with data, not panic.

costOfInaction.description: Continuing a stalled strategy burns $85K/mo in misallocated capital
costOfInaction.basis: SMB segment drag + below-target growth rate = value destruction

steps.title: Growth rate below target for 2+ quarters
steps.completedText: Growth on track, no pivot trigger
steps.description: If net new ARR stays below $1.5M for 2 quarters while competitors grow 20%+, the current GTM engine may be exhausted.

steps.title: Segment health audit
steps.description: Deep dive each segment: enterprise, mid-market, SMB. Which is growing? Which is dragging? Where's PMF strongest?

recommendations.label: Double down on Enterprise
recommendations.detail: Enterprise has 112% NRR, 6.2× LTV:CAC, and $7.2M ARR. This is the growth engine.

recommendations.label: Exit SMB entirely
recommendations.detail: SMB NRR is 96% (below breakeven), $0.52K ARPU declining 5%/quarter. This segment is destroying value.

recommendations.label: Pivot SMB to self-serve PLG
recommendations.impact: Automate onboarding, cut CSM cost to zero
recommendations.detail: Replace human-touch SMB with product-led growth. ARPU drops to $0.3K but cost-to-serve drops 90%.

steps.title: Competitive position assessment
steps.description: Are we losing deals because of features, pricing, or market fit? Win/loss analysis + competitor benchmarking.

steps.title: Board alignment on direction
steps.description: Present the data, the options, and a recommendation. Board must approve any strategic pivot before execution begins.

steps.title: Execute new strategy
steps.description: 90-day execution plan with weekly milestones. First milestone must show traction within 30 days or re-evaluate.

playbooks.title: Pricing Model Change
playbooks.subtitle: Restructure how you capture value
playbooks.description: When ARPU trends down, NRR drops below 100%, or competitors reprice — it's time to rethink pricing. This playbook evaluates whether a pricing change fixes the unit economics or just masks a product problem.

costOfInaction.description: Underpriced tiers leave $62K/mo on the table while NRR erodes
costOfInaction.basis: Enterprise ARPU 6.8× SMB but pricing gap is only 4× — value capture leak

steps.title: Pricing health diagnostic
steps.description: Are customers chricing on price or value? Analyze win/loss, NPS verbatims, and segment-level NRR. If NRR >110% everywhere, pricing isn't the problem — product is.

recommendations.label: Raise Enterprise prices 25-40%
recommendations.detail: Enterprise NRR is 112% and LTV:CAC is 6.2×. Customers are getting 6× the value they pay for. Price to value.

recommendations.label: Introduce usage-based tier for SMB
recommendations.impact: Replace flat $0.52K ARPU with consumption pricing
recommendations.detail: SMB NRR at 96% means flat pricing doesn't capture growth. Usage-based aligns cost with value. Heavy users pay more, light users stay.

recommendations.label: Kill the free/cheap tier entirely
recommendations.impact: Raise SMB floor from $0.52K to $0.8K minimum
recommendations.detail: Bottom 40% of SMB accounts use <$200/mo in value. They cost more in support than they pay. Minimum price gate filters tire-kickers.

steps.title: Competitive pricing benchmark
steps.description: Where do we sit vs competitors? If we're 40%+ below market, we're leaving money on the table. If we're above, we need to justify with product differentiation.

steps.title: Model new pricing tiers
steps.description: Design the new pricing structure. Three models under evaluation: hybrid seat + usage, pure usage-based, or value-based tiered. Each has different revenue and churn implications.

liveValue._note: No live metric — this is a design step

recommendations.label: Hybrid: seat-based Enterprise + usage SMB
recommendations.impact: Best of both — predictable base + growth capture
recommendations.detail: Enterprise stays per-seat (they want predictability). SMB moves to usage (they grow organically). Mid-market gets hybrid: base seats + usage overage.

recommendations.label: Pure usage-based across all tiers
recommendations.impact: Maximum growth alignment, but revenue volatility
recommendations.detail: Every customer pays for what they use. Upside: heavy users pay more automatically. Downside: revenue becomes lumpy, harder to forecast, finance team pushes back.
recommendations.runwayImpact: +2.1mo if usage grows, -0.8mo if it drops

recommendations.label: Value-based tiers (Good / Better / Best)
recommendations.impact: Clear upgrade path, proven SaaS model
recommendations.detail: 3 tiers with feature gating. Simplest to sell and understand. Risk: doesn't capture usage growth within a tier. Works best when feature differentiation is strong.

steps.title: Grandfather existing customers
steps.description: Decision: do existing customers keep old pricing forever, for 12 months, or migrate immediately? Each option has different churn and revenue impact.

resolutionOptions.label: Grandfather permanently
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: $1.8M/yr revenue gap vs new customers on new pricing

resolutionOptions.label: 12-month grandfather period
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Balanced — gives customers time to adapt, closes gap in year 2

resolutionOptions.label: Immediate migration with 20% discount
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Fastest revenue uplift but risky with Enterprise accounts

crossPortalLink.label: View at-risk accounts for churn modeling

steps.title: Board approval + launch
steps.description: Present the pricing model, revenue projection, and churn risk to the board. Get approval, then roll out with a 90-day monitoring plan.

playbooks.title: Market Expansion
playbooks.subtitle: Enter new segments or geographies
playbooks.description: When your core market saturates or growth decelerates, expansion unlocks the next S-curve. This playbook gates the decision with TAM validation, GTM readiness, and resource commitment.

costOfInaction.description: Every month delayed = $95K in first-mover advantage lost to competitors
costOfInaction.basis: Competitor expansion velocity vs our growth ceiling in current segments

steps.title: Core market saturation analysis
steps.description: How much headroom is left? If enterprise penetration is <30% of TAM, expand there first. If core is >60% penetrated, new market is the play.

recommendations.label: Expand Enterprise to mid-market Enterprise (500-2000 employees)
recommendations.detail: Our ICP is 2000+ employees. The 500-2000 segment has similar needs but shorter sales cycles. Product is ready, GTM needs adaptation.

recommendations.label: Go international — UK/EU first
recommendations.detail: UK is easiest (English, similar buying patterns). EU requires localization. Total cost: $480K setup, 6 months to first revenue.

recommendations.label: Vertical expansion — add healthcare vertical
recommendations.detail: Healthcare has compliance requirements (HIPAA) but pays 2× ARPU. Need product work but zero GTM changes.

steps.title: TAM/SAM/SOM validation
steps.description: Validate the market size with bottom-up analysis. Not analyst reports — actual customer conversations, competitive mapping, and willingness-to-pay data.

steps.title: GTM readiness assessment
steps.description: Do we have the sales motion, marketing channels, and CS capacity for this expansion? Or do we need to build from scratch?

resolutionOptions.label: Piggyback on existing GTM
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Only works for adjacent segments, not new geos or verticals

resolutionOptions.label: Partner/distributor model
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Less control, margin sharing (30-40%), brand dilution risk

steps.title: Resource commitment + budget
steps.description: How much runway are we spending on this? The bear scenario must stay above 12 months with expansion investment included.

crossPortalLink.label: Model expansion cost against runway scenarios

steps.title: Board approval + launch plan
steps.description: 90-day launch plan with weekly milestones. First revenue target within 6 months. Kill switch if no traction at 9 months.

playbooks.title: M&A / Acquisition
playbooks.subtitle: Buy vs build decision framework
playbooks.description: When a feature gap, talent need, or competitive threat surfaces — should you acquire or build? This playbook evaluates targets, models integration cost, and gates the go/no-go with board-level rigor.

costOfInaction.description: Competitor acquires the target first = $120K/mo in lost competitive position
costOfInaction.basis: Feature gap causing deal losses + competitor consolidation risk

steps.title: Strategic rationale: what are we buying?
steps.description: Not 'a company' — a capability. What specific gap does this fill? Technology, talent, customer base, or market position? If you can't articulate it in one sentence, don't proceed.

recommendations.label: Acquire for technology (feature gap)
recommendations.detail: We're losing 23% of Enterprise deals because of missing offline/mobile capability. CTO estimates 14 months to build. Acquisition could deliver in 90 days.

recommendations.label: Acquire for talent (team lift)
recommendations.detail: Hiring 15 senior engineers takes 9+ months. Acqui-hire delivers day-one productivity. Premium: 30-50% above individual hire cost.

recommendations.label: Acquire for customers (market share)
recommendations.detail: Absorb a smaller competitor's customer base. Cross-sell our platform to their users. Risk: churn during migration.

steps.title: Target identification + initial diligence
steps.description: Shortlist 3-5 targets. Initial financial health check, product demo, team assessment. Kill any with >2 red flags.

steps.title: Financial modeling: buy vs build
steps.description: Full 3-year model: acquisition cost + integration + retention vs organic build cost + time-to-market. The bear scenario must still show >12mo runway post-acquisition.

resolutionOptions.label: All-cash deal
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Burns cash reserves, bear scenario drops to 9.2mo

resolutionOptions.label: Earnout structure
resolutionOptions.impact: Pay for performance, lowest upfront cost
resolutionOptions.tradeoff: Seller misalignment, integration friction, 18mo earnout period

crossPortalLink.label: Model acquisition cost against bear/base/bull scenarios

steps.title: Integration plan
steps.description: Day-1 integration plan: org structure, systems migration, customer communication, retention packages for key talent. 70% of acquisitions fail at integration, not valuation.

steps.title: Board vote + close
steps.description: Final board presentation: rationale, target, valuation, integration plan, risk factors. Unanimous approval required for deals >$2M.
